task_objective: Implement performance optimizations (Strategies 1, 2, 4) with strict verification gates
design_decisions: Strategy 1 (Multi-Connection Parallelism) was prototyped for DuckDB but caused deadlocks on in-memory tables during benchmarking. It was reverted and skipped per the plan.
Strategy 2 (SQL-Level Sanitization) was implemented by optimizing `_sanitize_matrix` to bypass loops when no non-finite values are present, resulting in a ~0.7s speedup.

Strategy 4 (Cardinality Sketches) was implemented as an optional flag `use_sketches` that uses `approx_nunique()` for DuckDB backends, resulting in a total ~2s speedup (4.3%) on the 10M row benchmark.
results: Final 10M row benchmark: 44.50s (v0.1.14 baseline: 46.49s). All verification tests pass. All changes merged to `dev` and pushed to remote.
